article_title: Amazon S3 Turns 20 Storing Over 500 Trillion Objects Across Hundreds of Exabytes as AWS Rewrites Core Components in Rust

content_preview.title: Amazon S3 Turns 20 Storing Over 500 Trillion Objects Across Hundreds of Exabytes as AWS Rewrites Core Components in Rust
content_preview.summary: Amazon S3 marks its 20th anniversary storing over 500 trillion objects across hundreds of exabytes, while AWS progressively rewrites core storage components in Rust for memory safety and performance.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Amazon Web Services celebrated the 20th anniversary of Amazon Simple Storage Service on March 14, a date chosen for the original 2006 launch because it falls on Pi Day. What began as a service running on roughly 400 storage nodes across 15 racks in three data centers with approximately one petabyte of total capacity and 15 Gbps of bandwidth has grown into what AWS describes as the backbone of the modern internet.

S3 now [stores more than 500 trillion objects, serves over 200 million requests pe...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent cloud infrastructure News article covering S3's 20th anniversary with strong technical detail on scale, API stability, Rust rewrites, and data lake evolution.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register confirms all major stats (500T objects, 200M req/s, hundreds of exabytes, 123 AZs/39 regions, original scale, Stormacq quote, Rust rewrites, 11-nines durability, public access default). SiliconANGLE confirms Andy Warfield VP role, million data lakes, AI workloads, Hadoop S3A connector, shared data foundation. AWS blog is a legitimate primary source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against sources. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.originality: No existing S3 anniversary coverage in the archive.
recommendations: aws.amazon.com is a primary source and should be on the allowlist
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. Previous REQUEST_CHANGES was for cross-contaminated summary (SMILE spacecraft text). Summary now correctly describes S3 anniversary. Article body was already publication-ready. Approved.
